matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: An observed NAMPT variant places this sample in the curated NAMPT research context. The bundled NAMPT database is focused on metabolism, inflammation, and disease-association research. Most current human variant interpretation at this locus is research grade and cohort dependent. Common NAMPT variants are usually interpreted as regulatory or biomarker-linked modifiers rather than as high-penetrance alleles.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: NAMPT is studied in metabolism, obesity, inflammation, cardiovascular disease, cancer, and aging.
The gene also links NAD salvage to sirtuin biology, stress responses, and systemic inflammatory signaling.
NAD metabolism and healthy-aging biology.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a NAMPT variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. NAMPT methylation is best interpreted as regulatory context around NAD-salvage capacity and inflammatory-metabolic signaling. It should be integrated with broader pathway state rather than read as a direct biomarker for any one common SNP. The bundled NAMPT database is focused on metabolism, inflammation, and disease-association research. Most current human variant interpretation at this locus is research grade and cohort dependent. Common NAMPT variants are usually interpreted as regulatory or biomarker-linked modifiers rather than as high-penetrance alleles.
cases.rationale: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.
research_focus: NAMPT is studied in metabolism, obesity, inflammation, cardiovascular disease, cancer, and aging.
The gene also links NAD salvage to sirtuin biology, stress responses, and systemic inflammatory signaling.
NAD metabolism and healthy-aging biology.
